threatgraph_indicators.description: A suspicious .NET module was loaded into the process. Adversaries may load malicious .NET assemblies into a process in order to conceal the execution of malicious payloads. Investigate the process and surrounding events.

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority
A process has accessed the Chrome SQL Login Data database and loaded a decryption module. This may be an attempt to steal Chrome passwords. Review the process tree.
Credential-related registry keys accessed
A process launched whose behavior is likely related to a potentially unwanted program (PUP). Review the file.
A process engaged in network activity with a remote destination known for malicious activity. Investigate events around the remote connection.
A process saved the Security Account Manager SAM or SYSTEM hive to disk. If this is unexpected, it likely indicates credential theft. Investigate the process tree.

A file meets the cloud-based machine learning lowest confidence threshold for malicious files. Detection is based on a high degree of entropy, packing, anti-malware evasion, or other similarity to known malware.
threatgraph_indicators.display_name: Machine Learning Identified Lowest Confidence Malicious File

threatgraph_indicators.description: A process has opened a handle to a Falcon process with excessive permissions requested. If this is unexpected, review the process tree and surrounding events.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.
A process made a suspicious change to the registry that might indicate a malicious persistence mechanism. Investigate the registry key.
A process engaged in network activity with a remote destination known for malicious activity. Investigate events around the remote connection.

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.

A process saved the Security Account Manager SAM or SYSTEM hive to disk. If this is unexpected, it likely indicates credential theft. Investigate the process tree.
A process launched whose behavior is likely related to a potentially unwanted program (PUP). Review the file.
Credential-related registry keys accessed
